items.title: International Olympic Committee urged to drop reported gender test plans for female athletes
items.summary_auto: More than 80 human rights and sport advocacy groups have called on the ‌International Olympic Committee to abandon reported plans to introduce universal genetic sex testing for female athletes and impose a blanket ban on transgender and intersex competitors.

items.title: Fisheries officials investigating video of sailors bragging about giant crab catch near Vancouver
items.summary_auto: Parts of Vancouver's harbour are off-limits to crabbing, and while the men say they have stopped in "Vancouver, Canada," it's not clear exactly where the vessel is anchored.

items.title: Electricity demand, natural gas production and renewable power expected to soar by 2050
items.summary_auto: Electricity demand is set to boom in Canada by 2050, according to new modelling from the national energy regulator released on Tuesday.

items.title: NHL's head of player safety defends punishment for hit that ended Auston Matthews's season
items.summary_auto: George Parros is standing by the NHL's decision to suspend Anaheim Ducks defenceman Radko Gudas five games for a knee-on-knee hit that injured Toronto Maple Leafs star Auston Matthews, a punishment that has been criticized as too lenient.

items.title: 'Prolonged atmospheric river' brings heavy rain to B.C. coast
items.summary_auto: Rainfall warnings have been announced for parts of B.C. 's coast, with the heaviest rain expected on Vancouver Island and the Central Coast. Environment Canada says localized flooding is possible.

items.title: Top U.S. counterterrorism official Joe Kent resigns over Trump's war on Iran
items.summary_auto: Joe Kent, the director of the National Counterterrorism Center, announced his resignation on Tuesday, saying he "cannot in good conscience" back the Trump administration's war in Iran.

items.title: IN PHOTOS | Daily life amid Cuba's nationwide blackout
items.summary_auto: Cuba faces a nationwide blackout as its energy and economic crises deepen and its power grid continues to crumble.

items.title: Thinking of moving to a more 'affordable' part of the country? Consider this
All Ontario drivers will be able to use HOV lanes in off-peak hours: transportation minister
items.summary_auto: Minister Prabmeet Sarkaria said in a statement Tuesday that allowing solo drivers to use high-occupancy vehicle lanes outside of busy times will help reduce gridlock on major highways. Doug Ford's government is looking to regulate the changes later this year.

items.title: What are the policies guiding Taser use in Alberta? The answers aren't easy to find
items.summary_auto: Police data and policy documents about how and when officers use Tasers are not always easy to get. Experts say that needs to change.

items.title: Parental mistrust, hostile interactions a growing concern for Alberta teachers
items.summary_auto: When CBC News asked teachers what we should pay attention to in education, some flagged the growing mistrust that’s hurting relationships with parents and in some cases growing volatile. Here’s what we learned.

items.title: U.S. government confirms Tesla and LG Energy Solution large battery deal
items.summary_auto: LG also owns and operates NextStar, a similar battery cell factory in Windsor, Ont.

items.title: N.B. government projects $6B added to debt, continued deficits through mandate
items.summary_auto: The finance minister had warned of a budget driven by difficult choices, but says talks about those options are still underway.

items.title: Holt government projects $6B added to debt, continued deficits through mandate
items.summary_auto: The finance minister had warned of a budget driven by difficult choices, but says talks about those options are still underway.

items.title: Canada's benchmark home price fell 4.8% in February compared to last year, says CREA
items.summary_auto: National homes sales edged down in February from the previous month, and the benchmark price of a home fell by 4.8 per cent compared to the same time last year, according to data from the Canadian Real Estate Association.

items.title: Israel says it killed Iranian militia head, security chief amid 'wide-scale' strikes on Tehran
items.summary_auto: Israel's defence minister said that its military killed Iran's security chief and the head of its Basij militia in overnight airstrikes as it conducted a "wide-scale wave of strikes" across Tehran on Tuesday.

items.title: N.L. restaurants being squeezed, say loan guarantee program no use
items.summary_auto: Last spring, the Newfoundland and Labrador government announced a loan guarantee program for restaurants — but so far, no businesses have tapped into it.

items.title: An bhfuil Gaeilge agat? Why the Irish language is ‘having a moment’
items.summary_auto: Do you speak Irish? A growing number of people are picking up Ireland’s native language, both inside the country and here in Canada.

items.title: OPP investigation finds no wrongdoing from Toronto police officers at Umar Zameer trial
items.summary_auto: An Ontario Provincial Police (OPP) investigation examining the conduct of multiple Toronto police officers, launched after the judge at a high-profile murder trial accused responding officers of lying and collusion, has found no evidence of wrongdoing.

items.title: Her body was left on a couch for 6 months. Family, advocates say the system failed her
items.summary_auto: Two brothers avoided jail time after they admitted to keeping the body of Alasie Tukkiapik on their couch for six months in 2023. As the court case comes to a close, advocates and family members say the authorities failed the 41-year-old Inuk woman.

items.title: He came to Canada with a dream. RCMP say he became a victim of labour trafficking
items.summary_auto: The RCMP in N.S. have charged a man with a form of human trafficking known as labour trafficking, which they say is of growing concern across Canada. Labour trafficking calls to the Canadian Human Trafficking Hotline held steady for an average of 24 cases a year from 2020 to 2022, jumping to 57 calls in 2023, and 100 in 2024, according to a report produced by the Canadian Association to End Human Trafficking.

items.title: Another Loblaw store fined $10K for promoting imported food as Canadian. Sobeys could be next
items.summary_auto: The Canadian Food Inspection Agency (CFIA) is cracking down on grocery stores that promote imported food as Canadian. So far this year, the federal food regulator has issued two fines to Loblaw-owned grocery stores for this type of violation. The CFIA has also launched an investigation into labelling and advertising practices at grocer Sobeys’ head office.

items.title: Why allies aren't leaping to Trump's aid in Strait of Hormuz
items.summary_auto: U.S. President Donald Trump is struggling to persuade other countries to help protect commercial shipping through the Strait of Hormuz — a development analysts say is partly the result of how he has treated allies since returning to the White House last year.

items.title: Scott Pilgrim returns to brawl on the digital streets of Toronto in new video game
items.summary_auto: Toronto cartoonist Bryan Lee O'Malley continues to mark his comic book character Scott Pilgrim's 20th anniversary with the release of a new retro-styled video game.

items.title: Afghanistan accuses Pakistan of deadly strike on Kabul hospital that it says killed hundreds
items.summary_auto: Afghanistan accused Pakistan of targeting a hospital for drug users in the Afghan capital late Monday, saying the airstrike had killed at least 400 people. It marked a dramatic escalation of a conflict that began late last month and has seen repeated cross-border clashes as well as airstrikes inside Afghanistan. International calls for a ceasefire have gone unheeded.

items.title: B.C. hires more than 400 U.S. health-care workers in 1-year recruitment blitz
items.summary_auto: The numbers from B.C.’s Ministry of Health show 414 U.S. health-care workers accepted jobs in the province between March 2025 and January 2026.

items.title: Trump expects to have the 'honour' of taking Cuba
items.summary_auto: Calling Cuba a 'failed nation' with a 'nice landscape,' U.S. President ‌Donald Trump said Monday that ⁠he thinks ‌he will have the 'honour' of ⁠taking ⁠Cuba, adding he could do anything he wants with it.
